  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,

    "R1.3": 0.7,

    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Open-ended 'what do you think?' on a research excerpt naturally invites assessment of whether the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim are coherently framed and credible.",
    "R1.2": "Logical consistency is a primary target of any general intellectual opinion on research; the artifact contains potentially inconsistent premises (e.g., calling Hannun et al. 2014 'the most recent' competitor while framing temperature scaling as the current de facto standard).",
    "R1.3": "The artifact makes quantitative performance claims (ECE 3.1% vs 5.8%, Figure 3 reliability diagrams) that a 'what do you think?' response should evaluate for internal coherence and plausibility.",
    "R1.4": "The paper asserts a 'first systematic study' novelty claim; assessing whether that delta over prior work is defensible is a natural obligation when asked for a general opinion on a research contribution.",
    "R1.7": "Hannun et al. (2014) is described as 'the most recent confidence-aware ASR model,' which is a potentially serious citation/scholarship error; flagging reference accuracy problems is central to any honest opinion on this text."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Are you seeking a technical peer-review of the scientific claims, methodology, and novelty (which would make R1.2, R1.3, R1.4 load-bearing), or feedback on writing quality and prose clarity (which would make R1.6 load-bearing instead)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: Open-ended 'what do you think?' on a research excerpt naturally invites assessment of whether the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim are coherently framed and credible.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is a primary target of any general intellectual opinion on research; the artifact contains potentially inconsistent premises (e.g., calling Hannun et al. 2014 'the most recent' competitor while framing temperature scaling as the current de facto standard).
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes quantitative performance claims (ECE 3.1% vs 5.8%, Figure 3 reliability diagrams) that a 'what do you think?' response should evaluate for internal coherence and plausibility.
rationale.R1.4: The paper asserts a 'first systematic study' novelty claim; assessing whether that delta over prior work is defensible is a natural obligation when asked for a general opinion on a research contribution.
rationale.R1.7: Hannun et al. (2014) is described as 'the most recent confidence-aware ASR model,' which is a potentially serious citation/scholarship error; flagging reference accuracy problems is central to any honest opinion on this text.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you seeking a technical peer-review of the scientific claims, methodology, and novelty (which would make R1.2, R1.3, R1.4 load-bearing), or feedback on writing quality and prose clarity (which would make R1.6 load-bearing instead)?
